text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 44 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  The list of recently selected templates is user specific.  To search type the name of the required template, click , and then select the template from the search results.  Navigate the folder hierarchy in the Navigator pane, and then select the template.  Click the Details tab when a template is selected to view information about the template.  Click OK.  Complete the Consultation Properties screen, and then click OK .  Complete the selected template.  If the template has more than one page, select the required page number in the right-hand pane and then complete the screen.  To view data that has previously been added to the patient's care record using a template, click .  This icon is only displayed beside data if a previous entry exists.  The Trend screen is displayed, showing a tabular and/or graphical view of any previous entries.  If required, click Tabular . Each entry for the selected data is displayed, including the date when the data was added.  To edit the consultation properties from within the template, click Date/Consulter/Place on the ribbon, and then edit the Consultation Properties screen as required.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  If you want a user to be able to record consultation data under their own name, make sure they're configured as a consulter.  Click Save .  The data is added to the patient's care record. NOTE: Users must not enter clinical data as free text. Save a consultation When you have completed a consultation with all relevant data save the consultation using the Save Button on the consultation ribbon, this will close the current consultation for the patients. Edit a consultation NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one  Access Consultations.  Click point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one using Patient Find.  The Consultations screen is displayed. o Do one of the following:  In the Date Navigator, select the consultation you want to edit.  In the consultation main pane, click any detail of the consultation you want to edit. o On the Consultations ribbon, click Edit Consultation .  On the Consultation Edit screen, edit the consultation as required, and then click Save on the ribbon. You can view the audit trail of a consultation that has been edited by double-clicking beside the required consultation to open the Audit Trail Viewer screen.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Communications within EMIS Web Workflow Manager The Workflow manger is the area where all pending work for the practice is stored. There are various areas:- Tasks, Lab Reports, Medicine Management, GP2GP, Document Management, Referral Management, Test Requests and Report Management. You will be limited to seeing various headings, this will depending on whether you are a global viewer or only viewing Task that have been assigned to you.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 45 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Tasks The Tasks module within Workflow Manager enables you to:  Send requests for action to one or more other users.  Manage the tasks you have sent to other users, and the tasks you have received from other users. There are many different types of task available in EMIS Web, grouped into two main types:  Patient tasks: used for actions or information linked to specific patients.  Admin tasks: used for general administration, and not related to specific patients. If you customise the quick access toolbar and add the Add Task option, you can then use this option to add tasks from anywhere in EMIS Web. To access Tasks, click point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane. The Tasks screen is displayed, showing your To-Do List by default. Tasks screen and ribbon The Tasks screen consists of the following main sections:  Task categories are listed in the navigation (left-hand) pane: Category Section Contains Task Management To-Do List Tasks requiring action. The first number in brackets represents the number of tasks on the list, and the second number represents the number of those tasks due today.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: For example, (3, 1) in brackets indicates that there are three tasks in total, one of which is due today. Sent Tasks Tasks that you have sent. Completed Tasks Completed Completed tasks. Deleted Deleted tasks. Archived Archived tasks. Patient Access Messages Inbox Messages sent to your organisation using Patient Access.  When you select a task category in the navigation pane, tasks in the selected category are listed in the top right-hand pane.  When you select a task in the top right-hand pane, further information about the selected task is displayed in the bottom right-hand pane. Tasks icons and indicators To help you identify task types and manage tasks, the following indicators are used on the Tasks screen: Icon or indicator Indicates Red text in the Due Date column Overdue tasks. High priority tasks (displayed at the top of the list). Completed sent tasks. Tasks generated from pathology/lab reports. Tasks that have been replied to. Tasks that have been forwarded to another user. Tasks with notes attached to them. To read the note, click the icon. Tasks owned by a user other than the currently logged-on user. To view a tooltip showing the task owner’s name, hover over the icon.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 46 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) If you hover over any item count in the navigation pane, e.g. (9, 1), a tooltip is displayed to explain the meaning of the numbers shown. The first number is the total tasks in the folder, the second is the number of priority tasks in the folder. When you access Tasks, a ribbon is displayed at the top of the screen. Guide to ribbon options: Section Option Use to View Workflow View Filter tasks by their owner. Admin Config Access the Workflow Manager Configuration screen, to configure Tasks settings. Actions Change Owner Change task owners. Add Note Add notes to tasks. Use notes for non-essential information only, because the information in notes added to tasks is not auditable, and if you reply to or forward a task with a note attached, the note is not sent with the reply. Delete Note Delete notes from tasks. View History View the history of tasks. High Priority Change the priority of tasks. A red arrow is displayed beside high priority tasks, and they are displayed at the top of the list. Print Print selected tasks and/or task lists. Task Add Task Add new tasks. Task Filter Filter Apply filters to Tasks, so that only certain tasks are displayed.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: The filters available are:  For the To-Do List: Admin Tasks, Patient Tasks.  For Sent Tasks: Incomplete Tasks, Complete Tasks. Clear Filter Remove any filter applied to task lists. Task Menu Open Open tasks and view their details on the Workflow Manager Task screen. Reply Reply to tasks. You can reply to one or more senders. An icon is displayed beside tasks that have been replied to. Forward Forward tasks to other users. An icon is displayed beside tasks that have been forwarded. Complete Complete tasks, with or without adding notes to them. Delete Task Delete tasks. Batch Processing Complete and archive multiple tasks. Note: If necessary you can undo a completed batch of tasks. Actions Archive Tasks Archive tasks. You can only archive completed tasks; this option is only displayed when you select Completed in the navigation pane, or select a completed sent task (indicated by a green tick ). Activate Tasks Activate or reactivate tasks that were completed in error and need further action. Task Actions Make Appointment Access the Find Appointments screen and book an appointment for the patient that the task relates to. This option is only available for patient task types. View Report View the pathology/lab report that a task relates to.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: This option is only available for tasks added in Lab Reports. An icon is displayed beside tasks generated from pathology reports.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 47 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) View Attachment View the attachment that a task relates to. This option is only available for tasks generated when adding attachments to patients’ records. The options available on the ribbon may vary, depending on your role and the action you're taking. Adding new tasks There are many different types of task available in EMIS Web, grouped into two main types:  Patient tasks: used for actions or information linked to specific patients.  Admin tasks: used for general administration, and not related to specific patients. Add a patient task  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  On the ribbon, click Add Task .  Beside the Task Type field on the Task Details screen, click and select the required task type. The list displayed depends on which task types are configured as available.  Beside the Patient field, do one of the following: o Click and select the current patient's name. OR o Click , and then use the Patient Find screen to find and select the required patient.  In the Due Date field, type the date by which the task is to be actioned, or click and then select the required date.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: The default is today's date.  Beside the To field, click and then use the Find Teams or Users screen to select the required recipient. o If you have configured a default recipient for the selected task type, that recipient is displayed in the To field. However, you can change the recipient, if required.  If required, and if you are sending the task to more than one recipient, select All Recipients to Action Task . o If you select this option, a separate task is sent to each selected recipient, and they must all action the task.  In the Notes field, type details of the action required. The maximum number of characters you can type in this field is 8000. A counter at the bottom of the screen indicates the number of characters you have used.  If required, select Urgent .  Click Send . The task is sent to each selected recipient and displayed in their To-Do List. A separate task for each recipient is displayed in your Sent Tasks list. o A red arrow is displayed beside high priority tasks, and they are displayed at the top of the list. Add an admin task  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  On the ribbon, click Add Task .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  Beside the Task Type field on the Task Details screen, click and select the required task type. The list displayed depends on which task types are click configured as available.  In the Subject field, type the subject of the task.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 48 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) o If a patient is already selected, the name of this field is displayed as 'Patient' until you select a non- patient task type in the Task Type field.  In the Due Date field, type the date by which the task is to be actioned, or click and then use the calendar to select the required date. The default is today's date.  Beside the To field, click , and then use the Find Teams or Users screen to select the required recipient. o If you have configured a default recipient for the selected task type, that recipient is displayed in the To field. However, you can change the recipient, if required.  If required, and if you are sending the task to more than one recipient, select All Recipients to Action Task . o If you select this option, a separate task is sent to each selected recipient, and they must all action the task.  In the Notes field, type details of the action required. o The maximum number of characters you can type in this field is 8000. A counter at the bottom of the screen indicates the number of characters you have used.  If required, select Urgent .  Click Send . The task is sent to each selected recipient and displayed in their To-Do List.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: A separate task for each recipient is displayed in your Sent Tasks list. o A red arrow is displayed beside high priority tasks, and they are displayed at the top of the list. Change the owner of a task  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click the required task category.  In the right-hand pane, click the task you want to assign to a different owner.  Do one of the following: o On the Tasks ribbon, click Change Owner in the Actions Section , and then select the required user from the list of recently selected users. OR o On the Tasks ribbon, click Change Owner , and then use the Find Teams or Users screen to select the required user or team.  The names of users who are out of the office are greyed out in both these places.  The task is assigned to the selected user or team. Add a note to a task Use notes for non-essential information only, because the information in notes added to tasks is not auditable, and if you reply to or forward a task with a note attached, the note is not sent with the reply.  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  In the navigation pane, click the required task category.  In the right-hand pane, click the task you want to add a note to.  On the ribbon, click Add Note .  On the screen displayed, type the text of the note, and then click Close . The following changes are made: o The note is attached to the task, but is not part of the main body of the task in the Notes field. o On the Tasks screen, an icon is displayed beside the task; all senders and recipients can view the note, by clicking the icon. o In the Diary section of Care Record, an icon is displayed beside any tasks in the Current Items section and the Tasks section that have notes attached to them. You can hover your cursor over the

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 49 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Details column of the tasks list to view tooltips showing any free text that was typed in the Notes field on the User Task Details screen when the tasks were added.  To remove a note from a task, select the task, and then click Delete Note on the ribbon. View the history of a task  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click the required task category.  In the right-hand pane, click the required task. o On the ribbon, click View History . The Task History screen is displayed, showing the history of the task. o To close the Task History screen, click Close . Reply to a task You can only reply to tasks in the To-Do List. You cannot reply to cross-organisation tasks if the creator organisation does not accept tasks.  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click To-Do List .  In the right-hand pane, click the task that you want to reply to.  On the ribbon, click Reply and select one of the following: o Reply to Sender , if the task was sent by one user.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o Reply to All , if the task was sent by more than one user and you want to reply to all the senders. The User Task Details screen is displayed, with some details already completed.  If required, in the Due Date field, type or select a different date. The default is today’s date.  If required, beside the To field, click , and then use the Find Teams or Users screen to select a different recipient. The default is the owner(s) of the task.  If required, select All Recipients to Action Task .  In the Notes field, type your reply.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 50 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) o The maximum number of characters you can type in this field is 8000. A counter at the bottom of the screen indicates the number of characters you have used.  If required, select Urgent .  Do one of the following: o To send the reply and complete the task, click Send & Complete . The reply is sent to the selected recipient(s) and the task is moved to the Completed category. OR o To send the reply without completing the task, click Send . The reply is sent to the selected recipient(s), and another task is created for the original sender, based on your reply. The task also remains on your To-Do List.  An icon is displayed beside tasks that have been replied to. Forward a task You can only forward tasks in the To-Do List.  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click To-Do List .  In the right-hand pane, click the task that you want to forward to another user.  On the ribbon, click Forward . The User Task Details screen is displayed, with some details already completed.  If required, in the Due Date field, type or select a different date. The default is today’s date.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  Beside the To field, click , and then use the Find Teams or Users screen to select the required recipient(s).  If required, select All Recipients to Action Task .  If required, in the Notes field, type any additional information. The maximum number of characters you can type in this field is 8000. A counter at the bottom of the screen indicates the number of characters you have used.  If required, select Urgent .  Do one of the following: o To forward and complete the task, click Send & Complete . The task is forwarded to the selected recipient(s) and moved to the Completed category. OR o To forward the task without completing it, click Send . The task is forwarded to the selected recipient(s), and another task is created for the original sender. The task also remains on your To-Do List.  An icon is displayed beside tasks that have been forwarded. Complete a task You can only complete tasks in the To-Do List. Hold down Shift + left-click or Ctrl + left-click to select multiple tasks.  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click To-Do List .  In the right-hand pane, click the task that you want to complete.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  To complete the task immediately, click Complete on the Tasks ribbon and select Complete Task . The task is moved to the Completed category.  To add a note to the task and then complete it, click Complete on the ribbon and select Complete Task with Note . o On the Task Description screen, type the required information, and then click OK . The task is moved to the Completed category, with the note attached.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 51 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Delete a task You can only delete tasks from the To-Do List. Hold down Shift + left-click or Ctrl + left-click to select multiple tasks.  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click To-Do List .  In the right-hand pane, click the task that you want to delete.  On the ribbon, click Delete Task .  On the Workflow Manager box that appears, click Delete Task . The task is moved to the Deleted category. Archive a task You can only archive completed tasks. Hold Shift + left-click or Ctrl + left-click to select multiple tasks.  Access Tasks. Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click one of the following: o Completed Tasks. o Sent Tasks.  A green tick is displayed beside completed sent tasks.  In the right-hand pane, click the required task.  On the ribbon, click Archive Tasks .  On the Task Action Confirmation screen, click OK . The task is moved to the Archived category. Print a task Any items that you print for dummy patients include a 'Dummy Patient' watermark.  Access Tasks.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click the required task category.  In the right-hand pane, select the task(s) you want to print.  On the ribbon, click Print and select Selected Task(s) .  If required, on the Print screen, change the printing options.  Click OK . Details of the selected task(s) are printed on your default printer. Print a task list  Access Tasks. o Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , and then click Tasks in the navigation pane.  In the navigation pane, click the required task category.  On the ribbon, click Print Task List .  If required, on the Print screen, change the printing options.  Click OK . A list of all tasks in the selected category is printed on your default printer. Change the priority of a task You can only change the priority of tasks on the To-Do List and Sent Tasks.  Access Tasks. Click , point to Workflow , select Workflow Manager , then click Tasks in the navigation pane.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 52 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  In the navigation pane, click one of the following: o To-Do List. o Sent Tasks.  In the right-hand pane, click the required task.  On the ribbon, click High Priority .  On the Change Task Priority screen, click Yes . The priority of the task changes: o If the task was previously a normal priority task, it changes to a high priority task. A red arrow is displayed beside high priority tasks, and they are displayed at the top of the list. o If the task was previously a high priority task, it changes to a normal priority task. Outstanding and overdue tasks A tasks toolbar is displayed at the top of every EMIS Web screen, just below the ribbon, showing your current/outstanding and overdue Workflow Manager tasks. The toolbar shows:  Your task count for Tasks and for the other Workflow Manager modules. If you are acting as a deputy for another user, their tasks are included in the task count.  A number in brackets, in red text, to indicate priority items.  For Lab Reports, the red number indicates tasks with abnormal results. Click the Tasks link under the ribbon, or the Tasks option in the navigation pane, to access Tasks and action the tasks.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: In Tasks, the Due Date is displayed in red if the task is overdue. Task escalation If active tasks that are assigned to users or teams are not completed within a specified number of hours (the escalation interval, i.e. the amount of time in hours that can pass after the due date of a task before the task is considered overdue), the tasks are escalated to the administrators. You can configure administrators and escalation intervals for routine and urgent tasks on the Workflow Manager Configuration screen. If a task would become overdue on a day that is not a working day for your organisation, escalation takes place on the next working day. A task escalation report is produced each day, and tasks remain in the report until completed. If an active report already exists for the user or the administrators, EMIS Web will update the existing report rather than creating a new one. The due date of a task is one or other of the following:  The date specified in the Due Date field on the User Task Details screen when adding the task.  The date when the task was created, if no date was specified in the Due Date field on the User Task Details screen.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: A task is considered inaccessible if at least one of the following conditions applies to all users assigned to the task:  The user is not currently contracted (i.e. their contract has ended or has not yet started) or has been deleted.  The user does not have does not have the correct RBAC activity in their role profile to access Workflow Manager.  The user does not have the correct RBAC activity in their role profile to process tasks of that type. Information governance notifications If you are a privacy officer for your organisation, you need to receive the following notifications relating to information governance: Task/notification type Trigger action

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 53 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Legitimate relationship notification A user accesses an inactive patient's record outside the grace period configured in Patient Archive. Unarchived patient record notification A user unarchives a patient in Patient Archive. Inactive records accessed in Reporting A user accesses an inactive patient's records from search results. To make sure you receive these notifications, you need to be configured as the default recipient for these task types in the task default settings. Your organisation must have at least one user or team as its privacy officer to receive these notifications, and the privacy officer must not be any patient's usual GP. Screen messaging You can send screen messages to other users from anywhere in EMIS Web. New users are added to the list of available users automatically, but can take up to 24 hours to appear due to waiting for the messaging server to refresh. You can send messages to users who are online, offline, or away. The colour of the circular icon beside the user's name indicates their current status. Icon colour Status Green Online (user is logged into EMIS Web). Grey Offline (user is not logged into EMIS Web).

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Yellow Away (user has locked their session or is in a consultation).  A user's status is updated immediately, so there is no need to refresh.  A green flag is displayed in the bottom right-hand corner of the screen when a user is available to receive a message. A yellow flag is displayed when the user is in a consultation. If Screen Messaging is unavailable, a warning message will appear stating: Screen Messaging is currently unavailable because EMIS Web Cannot connect to the messaging server. Try again later. If you receive this message, try logging out and back in, or try using Screen Messaging again later. Send a screen messages  Screen messages should not be used for clinical emergencies.  If urgent clinical assistance is required then the Panic Alerts function should be used instead.  On the quick access toolbar, click the screen message icon to display the Send Screen Message screen.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 54 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Send Screen message screen o In the Subject field, type the subject of the message, up to a maximum of 100 characters. o In the Message field, type the message you want to send, up to a maximum of 500 characters. o If the message is urgent, select Urgent . Urgent messages trigger a separate alert, displayed in the middle of the user’s screen. Users can click View to read the message, or Close to view later. Urgent Message screen  Urgent messages are also displayed in the same yellow alert bar as normal messages. Clicking the link will launch the message viewer, which enables users to view, reply to and/or discard any messages they have received.  If there are urgent messages, an Urgent column is displayed on the left of the Messages section to indicate which messages are urgent. If there are no urgent messages, the additional column is not displayed.  Select the user(s) you want to send the screen message to. o Users can be displayed alphabetically, or by job role. You can show/hide offline users and also use the Find User section by typing in the first few letters of the forename or surname. The maximum number of recipients per message is 100.  Click Send .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: The message is sent to each selected user and displayed in a yellow alert bar above the patient précis on the EMIS Web screen they are currently viewing. Only one alert bar is displayed, with a link to the message(s). Clicking this link will launch the message viewer which enables users to view, reply to and/or discard any of the messages they have received.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 55 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) o If a user tries to close the alert bar using the red cross, a warning is displayed, as this will delete the messages permanently.  There is no audit trail available for sent messages. Reply to a screen message If you receive a screen message, you can do any of the following:  Send a reply to the sender only o Select the message and click Reply . o The Send Screen Message screen is displayed, with the sender’s name selected. o In the Message field, type your reply. o Click Send . OR  Send a reply to everyone who received the message o Select the message and click Reply All . o The Send Screen Message screen is displayed, with all recipients' names selected. o In the Message field, type your reply. o Click Send . Discard the message Select the message and click Discard . If you discard a message, it cannot be retrieved. Consultations (Clinical) Configure the homepage to show appointments You can configure the homepage with your own personal preferences.  At the top right-hand corner of the homepage, click Configure Homepage .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  On the Configure Homepage screen, to display appointments for a selected user/location on your homepage, do the following: o In the Appointments section, select Show Appointments on homepage . o In the Session Holder field, click and then use the Find Session Holder screen to select the required session holder or location. Double-click the selected session holder/location, to select and then click OK . Automatically open a consultation on Send in You can configure the Appointment Book to automatically open a consultation when a patient is sent in for your profile.  Access the Appointment Book. o Click point to Appointments , select Appointment Book , and then on the ribbon, click Appts Config . The Appointments Configuration screen is displayed  In the left-hand pane, under User Options, select the General tab.  In the New Consultation section, click Start new Consultation next to the On a registered patient send in option. Consultations are the most common way of adding information to patients' care records. There are three main types of consultation available in EMIS Web. The type(s) you use must be decided at organisation level, because the decision affects the whole organisation. The three types of consultation are:

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 56 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  Header-based: The consultation type most EMIS users will be familiar with and the one most suited for GP organisations and community practitioners.  Quick note consultation: A free-text only version; you can't use quick notes to record coded information.  Template consultation: This type is not generally suited to GP organisations. To add a consultation, you must have RBAC activity B0380 in your role profile, and be configured as a consulter in the Role section of the Add User or Edit User screen in Organisation Configuration. Access the New Consultation screen.  Click , point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o On the ribbon, click Add and select the required consultation style. You will be prompted to complete the C onsulta tion Properties screen first. o Add the following information to the consultation, as required: Consultation properties When you add a consultation to a patient's care record, use the Consultation Properties screen to specify key features of the consultation, for example, the date, the consulter and the location.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: You can only select someone as a consulter who has already been configured as a consulter in organisation configuration; they will not show in the picking list unless they've been setup as a consulter. You can also store the settings on the Consultation Properties screen as the default for your session; this means you only have to complete this screen once in a session. Complete the Consultation Properties screen  Access the New Consultation screen. o Click , point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . If you don't have a patient selected, you're prompted to choose one. o On the ribbon, click Add and select the required consultation style.  If required, type the required date in the Date field.  In the Time field, type the required time.  If you don't want to specify a time, highlight the time, and then press DELETE .  Do one of the following: o Record a consultation at your organisation. o Select My Organisation , and then complete the Consulter and Location fields. o The Location field is automatically populated with your organisation's main location. OR o Record a consultation external to your organisation. o Select External Organisation , and then complete the Organisation and External Consulter fields.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o In the Consultation Type field, click and select the required consultation type. o If required, beside Extended Properties, click to add further details:  In the Travel field, type the required number of minutes.  In the Miles field, type the number of miles travelled.  In the Duration field, type the required number of minutes.  If another HCP was present during the consultation, in the Accompanying HCP field, click , and select the required user. o If required, select Store as default for this session , and then click OK .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 57 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) If you select this option, the same details are automatically added for all consultations in the session. If you are backdating a consultation, you need to either leave the option blank to make sure the date/time is not recorded incorrectly for other consultations, or edit the consultation properties for other consultations and select today's date/time. Consultation Properties screen highlighting the Store as default for this session option New Consultation screen and ribbon You can click the contact slider at the bottom of the screen to change the level of detail displayed in the bottom pane (displaying the previous ten consultations). To open a document attached to a previous consultation while you have a new consultation open, double-click . o If you cannot see the slider look for the Latest Contacts Tab at the bott om left hand side of the screen and click this tab. The New Consultation screen with the contact slider in the bottom pane If you selected a header based consultation, a list of headings is displayed on the left-hand side of the screen. Use these headings to add different information to the patient’s consultation.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: The column on the right-hand side of the New Consultation screen contains two tabs: Summary and Resource. Depending which option is highlighted, depends on the information shown here.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 58 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) NOTE: When you add a new header based consultation or quick note, the New Consultation ribbon is displayed. Add a New Consultation You can now cut, copy or paste text in an open consultation. Highlight the text and then do one of the following:  Right click and select Cut, Copy or Paste.  Press Ctrl+X to cut.  Press Ctrl+C to copy.  Press Ctrl+V to paste. Add a New problem NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. From the Problem section of the Consultations screen, select the required problem, or start to type the problem condition or clinical term. As you type, a list of problems matching your criteria is displayed. The code list is based on commonality (i.e. the more often you select a code, the higher up the list it moves).  To add a problem that is not in the list, press F4 to view a more extensive list of codes, and then select the required problem code.  When you select a problem code, the default significance details for the problem are displayed, underneath the problem. o If required, click the Drop down arrow next to Active Problem, and then select one of the following:  Active problem  Health Administration.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  Past/Ended Problem. o If required, click the Drop down arrow next to Minor, and then select Significant or Minor . o If required, click the Drop down arrow next to First Episode, and then select one of the following:  New Episode  Review  Flare Up  End Episode o If required Click the Drop down arrow next to Remains active for 28 days, and then select one of the following:  7 Days  14 Days  28 Days  3 Months  6 Months  1 Year  Indefinitely  Custom (and then type the required number of days). o If you add a problem that is already in the patient's record, it is automatically added as a review. If you add a new problem in a consultation as a review and select an onset date other than today, the onset date is reset to today's date when any medication is added. o If required, select Set default duration for organisation . (option only available once you have set the activity to 3 months) The default duration is then automatically set for future problems added with the same code, although it’s still possible to amend the duration for each problem individually.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 59 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Consultation screen with the Set as default duration for organisation option highlighted o If required, you can manage related problems. Click Action , and then select one of the following:  Combine  Evolve  Group o The Select Problems to manage screen is displayed.  Select the problems which you want to Combine/Evolve/Group. Add Multiple Problems To record another problem that the patient has presented with click the Next Problem button on the toolbar this will add another section for you to be able to record the details for a different problem. To add another problem  Click Next Problem on the ribbon, and then follow the steps detailed in the New Problem section a bove. Add an Examination In the list on the left hand side of the screen select the option Examination, this will add an area for you to enter the examination details in the consultation area. Click on the down arrow next to the lightning strike this will show you the most recent that you have used. If the examination is not listed just enter the text in the box provided. .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 60 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) Referral NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one.  From the New Consultation screen, press R , and then select either NHS e-Referral , Inbound Referral or Standard Outbound Referral as required. To use e-Referral Service functionality, you must log on to EMIS Web using your smartcard. o Complete the Inbound Referrals or Outbound Referrals screen, as required. The referral is added to the consultation, the Referrals screen and, if appropriate, the Referral Management section of Workflow Manager. Run a template NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one.  From the New Consultation screen, click Run Template on the ribbon.  On the Template Picker screen, do one of the following to select a template, and then click OK : o Select a template from the Recently Used list. o Type the name of the required template, click , and then select the template from the results. o In the Navigator pane, navigate the hierarchy, and then select the template.  To view details about a template, click the Details tab when the template is selected.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o Complete the template, and then click OK to add the information to the consultation. o To help you complete templates quicker, you can use TAB to move through the fields, and then use the SPACEBAR to select or deselect the tick boxes.  When completing a template with a diary entry, highlight the date field and enter either 3m (3months), 1y (1 year) to automatically change the date instead of using the calendar Add a document . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one.  From the New Consultation screen, Click Documents, and select from Create Letter , Attach Document or Attach Existing Document , as required.  Select one of the following depending on what option you selected at step 1: Create Letter: o Do one of the following:  To create a letter from a blank document, select New Document .  To create a letter from a template, select a previously used template, or click , and then use the Find Document Templates screen to select the required template.  Click OK . If you double-click the required option, the screen closes and the document opens.  If prompted, complete the Patient Letter Details screen.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o The document is displayed on the New Consultations screen, which consists of EMIS Web controls in the left-hand pane and your version of Microsoft Word in the main pane. Microsoft Word versions 2003, 2007 and 2010 are compatible with the EMIS Web system, providing you have the latest updates.  Compose the letter, as required.  You can use the tabs in the left-hand pane to add information from the patient’s care record. o In the left-hand pane, click File and select Save As . o On the Document Properties screen, in the Document Type field, click and select a previously used document type, or click , and then use the Code Selector screen to select a document type.  In the Source field, click and select a previously used source, or click , and then use the Find Service screen to select a source.  In the Document Title field, type an appropriate document title.  In the Date field, click and select a date for the document entry.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 61 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  If required, to create a consultation reference for the document, select Create a consultation for letter . o If you are using existing templates, default properties will be used when the document is saved. o Click OK to save the document and, if configured, create a consultation reference. Attach Document: o On the Open screen, locate the required document, and then click Open . o Complete the Attach Document screen, and then click File to attach the document to the consultation and display it on the Documents screen. Attach Existing Document: o On the Care Record Picker screen, select the box for the required document. Click OK to attach the document to the consultation and display it on the Documents screen. Add More Information  From the New Consultation screen, click the required heading.  On the Consultations screen, add the required information, and then press ENTER twice to return to the headings.  If you add weight and height information to a consultation, you are prompted to calculate and add the patient's BMI. If you edit weight and/or height information, you are then also prompted to recalculate the patient's BMI.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o You can record values with inequalities for codes, e.g. <, >, <=, >=, ~, ~=. For example, you can record a healthy human concentration of C-reactive proteins as <10 mg/L. You can also record negative values. o Inequality is not supported with height and weight values (metric or imperial). When you have completed the consultation, do one of the following:  On the ribbon, click Save .  On the ribbon, click Save as draft .  To cancel the consultation without saving, on the New Consultation ribbon, click Cancel , and then click Yes to confirm. Add a review consultation  Access the New Consultation screen. o Click , point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o On the ribbon, click Add and select the required consultation style.  To add a review problem to the consultation, select the problem and press P .  To select a problem from the list, double-click the required problem; this will display the default significance details for the problem.  Follow the procedure in the Additional problem section.  Refer to Add a new consultation for information on how to add information to a consultation.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  When you have complete the consultation, click Save on the ribbon to save the consultation and add the details to the Consultations screen. To cancel the consultation without saving, click Cancel on the ribbon, and then click Yes to confirm. Add a quick note You can add quick notes to consultations.  Access the New Consultation screen. o Click , point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o On the ribbon, click Add and select the required consultation style.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 62 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  Type the required information.  When you have completed the consultation, click Save on the ribbon to save the consultation and add the details to the Consultations screen. Prescribing & Issuing Medication Acute Medication In the Medication module, the screen is will appear in 3 sections, Acute, Repeats and Repeat Dispensing, but only if the patient has had these prescribed. Use the Add Drug screen to add one or more items of medication to a patient's record, issue any current medication and restart any past medication. You can also use the Add Drug screen to add further prescribing detail, such as dosage and quantity, prescription type, pharmacy and patient information, review date, days before next issue and authorising clinician. You can link medication to any of the patient's problems (for example, link an issue of Salbutamol to the problem Asthma).  Access Medication. o Click point to Care Record , and then select Medication . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o The Medication screen is displayed. o To include a reminder note on the prescription, click Patient Actions on the ribbon and select Send Reminder .

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: The reminder will be printed on the next prescription added, issued or reprinted for the patient. To configure the reminder note text, use the Medication Configuration screen: select Organisation Options , and then type the text in the Right Hand Side section.  On the ribbon, click Add Drug .  On the Add a Drug screen, type the name of the drug you want to prescribe, and then select the required item from the list. o As you type, the drugs that match your text (and their generic/trade equivalents) are displayed. o The order the drugs are displayed in the picking list depends on how frequently your practice has selected these items; e.g. if you type 'para' (for paracetamol), then the drugs you prescribe most frequently containing these letters are displayed at the top of the list.  To populate the Dosage and Quantity fields, do one of the following: o Select a previous dosage/quantity. To remove an incorrect dosage/quantity that you have previously added (displayed in blue), click Remove . You cannot remove any default recommendations (displayed in black). o In the appropriate fields, type a new dosage and quantity (maximum of 400 characters). o If you type an unrecognised dosage, the Dosage screen is displayed, prompting you to add details of the dosage.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Take care when completing this screen, as the information you add affects duration calculations. o Select a previous dosage, or select a new one, and then specify the quantity by clicking one of the items in the Pack Details pane. If the dosage is calculable, the duration is also specified. Note: If you selected Always Warn when Automatically Adjusting Pack Size on the Medication Configuration screen (Organisation Options, General) and you specify a quantity that is 90% of a complete pack size, you are prompted to change the quantity to issue the entire pack: click Yes to change the quantity, or click No to continue with the specified quantity Repeat Medication Issue medication  Access Medication.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 63 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) o Click , point to Care Record , and then select Medication . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o The Medication screen is displayed.  Access the Issue screen. o Use one of the following options: o If you are issuing existing medication, select the required medication on the Current Medication screen, and then click Issue (or Reauthorise and Issue ). o If you are adding new medication, the Issue screen is displayed when you click Issue after completing the Add a Drug screen.  If the item needs reauthorising, the Reauthorise Medication screen is displayed. o The Approve and Complete option on the Issue screen is disabled until you have reauthorised any medication that needs reauthorisation.  The Issue screen is displayed, with the patient précis showing the patient details that will be printed on the prescription (including EPS pharmacy information, if appropriate). o All issue types are displayed on the same screen, listed by type if there is more than one type. If the medication items will not all fit onto the screen, a scrollbar is included.  If required, deal with any warnings and/or safety alerts.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text:  If required, change the stamp doctor for this issue only, click To be signed by , and then use the Find Users screen to select the required stamp doctor.  If required, use the options located at the top and bottom of the screen, using the section below as a guide. o All controlled drugs are printed on the same prescription as normal medication. However, any medication that is dispensed by a hospital is printed on a separate prescription. Complete the issue Do one of the following:  To issue all items that need no further action, click Approve and Complete . This option is disabled if any item needs reauthorisation or is subject to a medium or high severity warning.  To approve and issue all items for a prescription type, click Approve and Next . EMIS Web automatically selects the next issue type, or you can click Next at the bottom of the screen to move to the next type. When you have approved all prescription types, the items are issued.  To send a prescription request, click Request . The Issue Request screen is displayed; complete the details, and then click Request . You can no longer edit medication from the medication screen if there is a pending request task. You now need to open the Workflow Task in Workflow Manager to edit the item.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Add data using a template  Access Care Record. o Click , Click Care Record . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one. o On the ribbon, click Add and select Data using Template . o On the Template Picker screen, to select a template, do one of the following:

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 64 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019)  The list of recently selected templates is user specific. o To search type the name of the required template, click , and then select the template from the search results. o Navigate the folder hierarchy in the Navigator pane, and then select the template.  Click the Details tab when a template is selected to view information about the template.  Click OK .  Complete the Consultation Properties screen, and then click OK .  Complete the selected template. o If the template has more than one page, select the required page number in the right-hand pane and then complete the screen.  To view data that has previously been added to the patient's care record using a template, click . o This icon is only displayed beside data if a previous entry exists. o The Trend screen is displayed, showing a tabular and/or graphical view of any previous entries.  If required, click Tabular . Each entry for the selected data is displayed, including the date when the data was added.  To edit the consultation properties from within the template, click Date/Consulter/Place on the ribbon, and then edit the Consultation Properties screen as required.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: o If you want a user to be able to record consultation data under their own name, make sure they're configured as a consulter.  Click Save . The data is added to the patient's care record. NOTE: Users must not enter clinical data as free text. Save a consultation When you have completed a consultation with all relevant data save the consultation using the Save Button on the consultation ribbon, this will close the current consultation for the patient. Edit a consultation  Access Consultations.  Click point to Care Record , and then select Consultations . NOTE: If you do not have a patient selected, you are prompted to choose one.  The Consultations screen is displayed.  Do one of the following: o In the Date Navigator, select the consultation you want to edit. o In the consultation main pane, click any detail of the consultation you want to edit.  On the Consultations ribbon, click Edit Consultation .  On the Consultation Edit screen, edit the consultation as required, and then click Save on the ribbon. You can view the audit trail of a consultation that has been edited by double-clicking this icon beside the required consultation to open the Audit Trail Viewer screen.

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Locking your session, logging off and closing EMIS Web You must lock your session every time you move away from your PC. This helps to reduce the risk of someone else using your session, either inadvertently or maliciously. Lock your session (no smartcard )  Click point to Session , and then select Lock Session . The EMIS Web Locked screen is displayed and EMIS Web is locked.  Do one of the following:

docTitle: LPUG0019 Emis Web Introduction Admin Clinical
text: Emis Web Introduction Admin /Clinical Page 65 of 66 Created/updated date(November 2019) o To unlock EMIS Web, type your password in the Password field, and then press ENTER .  If you type your password incorrectly three times, EMIS Web locks your account and you must ask your system administrator to unlock it. o To close EMIS Web, click Close EMIS Web , and then click Yes to confirm. Lock your session (with smartcard)  Remove your smartcard from the smartcard reader. EMIS Web locks and the GEM Authenticate screen is displayed.  To access EMIS Web, type your PIN in the Passcode field, and then select OK . Log off EMIS Web  Click point to Session , and then select Log Off . The Log Off screen is displayed.  Do one of the following: o To log off the current session, click Yes . o The session closes and the EMIS Web logon screen is displayed. o To close the screen and return to the current session, click No . Close EMIS Web  Click point to Session , and select Close Application . The EMIS Web is Closing screen is displayed.  Do one of the following: o To close EMIS Web, click Yes . o To return to EMIS Web, click No . User Guide Disclaimer: Please note that while we make every effort to keep this guide up to date.

text: 2023 Primary Care Safeguarding Handbook D Notifying a child’s safeguarding concern in Barking & Dagenham CONCERN ABOUT Abuse / Neglect / Safety of a child A: Physical or behavioural indication / sign B: Parent/carer discloses information that makes you suspect a child/YP is at risk C: A child/YP tells you they are being abused in some way Report immediately to PRACTICE Lead GP or Practice Manager (verbally or via Report Form) (NB In the absence of both, report to a GP Partner) PRACTICE Lead GP / Practice Manager To review content of concern by meeting with originator and gaining full facts (remind person highlighting concern of confidentiality, protection of identity to avoid any backlash) PRACTICE Lead GP to CONSIDER CONCERN Does the concern warrant reporting? Phone FIRST to discuss with the LA Children’s Services Assessment Team 020 8227 3811 020 8594 8356 (OOH) Provide full details of reasons why and record all information fully Contact the LA Children’s Services Assessment Team for further advice.

text:
The Riverside Practice

Absence Management Policy and Procedure

Introduction

The Company values good attendance and has a duty to maintain service delivery, minimise disruption and to ensure that unreasonable strain is not placed upon other employees through coping with additional workloads due to poor attendance.

There is no intention to penalise employees who are genuinely ill. However all absence must be monitored and the causes of absenteeism dealt with where possible. Obviously it is of no benefit to anyone to have genuinely sick employees struggling into work. That is not the aim of this policy. If you do have a problem, however, whether at work or home, staying away from work may not be the answer. A discussion with your line manager may help or they may be able to refer you to someone who can.

This policy does not form part of your contract and may be amended at any time.  The Company may also vary the procedures set out including any time limits, as appropriate in any case.

POLICY

can.

This policy does not form part of your contract and may be amended at any time.  The Company may also vary the procedures set out including any time limits, as appropriate in any case.

POLICY

Whilst the Company understands that there will be occasions where absences are unavoidable, it must focus on the effect that poor attendance levels have on the business.  By implementing this policy, the Company aims to strike a reasonable balance between the business needs and the genuine needs of the employees.

The aim of this policy is to secure a fair and consistent approach wherever reasonable and practical for the positive management of attendance.

By utilising this policy, the Company can identify trends and patterns in attendance and work with the employee to understand the reason for frequent or regular absence or appointments and support them moving forward.

WORKING FROM HOME

Where you have contracted a contagious illness (including COVID), the Company may allow you to work from home should you feel you are able to. Whether you choose to work from home or have sickness absence, you should stay away from the Company premises until you feel better.

Roles and Responsibilities

home should you feel you are able to. Whether you choose to work from home or have sickness absence, you should stay away from the Company premises until you feel better.

Roles and Responsibilities

Management have a direct responsibility for managing and monitoring attendance for team members and ensuring appropriate documents are produced on an employee’s return to work after a period of absence.

Employees are expected to:

attend work unless not fit to do so

report absence in accordance with the Company’s absence reporting procedure

communicate daily with the practice manager regarding their absence until they have received a statement of fitness for work (fit note) from their GP/Medical advisor, unless agreed otherwise with the practice manager.

maintain weekly contact with the practice manager while in receipt of a fit note, unless agreed otherwise with the practice manager.

complete the appropriate absence form or submit fit notes as required; and

Attend a return to work meeting on their return

Procedure for Reporting an Absence

otherwise with the practice manager.

complete the appropriate absence form or submit fit notes as required; and

Attend a return to work meeting on their return

Procedure for Reporting an Absence

If you are unable to attend work due to incapacity you must notify the practice manager in person, by telephone on the first day of absence (by TIME e.g. by 6:45am or one hour before of your contracted start time). If the practice manager is not available then a message should be left with Dr R Goel.

Personal contact is required at all times. Text messages; emails or messages left with work colleagues are not an acceptable method of reporting an absence. In exceptional circumstances, if you are unable to make a telephone call due to the nature of your sickness you should arrange for someone to make the call on your behalf, telling them the name of the appropriate person to contact and the reason you are unable to make the call personally.  You should ensure this person also provides the practice manager with a contact telephone number so that if necessary and appropriate, they can call you back later that day.

Personal contact is required at all times.

this person also provides the practice manager with a contact telephone number so that if necessary and appropriate, they can call you back later that day.

Personal contact is required at all times.

You must advise the practice manager of the reason for your absence, the expected duration, whether you have or intend on visiting your GP/Medical advisor and if any medication has been prescribed.

If your absence exceeds one day then you must call the practice manager each day to provide an update on your condition and anticipated return to work date until a fit note has been received (unless agreed otherwise with the practice manager).

Failure to report an absence may be regarded as unauthorised absence, which is unpaid and you may be subject to disciplinary action on your return to work.

Absence Documentation

You are required to provide/complete the relevant documentation specified below as appropriate in a timely manner in order to evidence your incapacity for work.

Absences lasting up to seven consecutive calendar days (including non-working days and weekends)

specified below as appropriate in a timely manner in order to evidence your incapacity for work.

Absences lasting up to seven consecutive calendar days (including non-working days and weekends)

You may ‘self-certify’ sickness absence which lasts up to and including seven consecutive calendar days. On your first day back to work, you must complete an absence form which serves to meet this requirement. The form must be signed by you and the relevant manager and attached to the return to work paperwork which will be retained on file.

Absences lasting for eight or more consecutive days (including non-working days and weekends)

and the relevant manager and attached to the return to work paperwork which will be retained on file.

Absences lasting for eight or more consecutive days (including non-working days and weekends)

From the 8th day of absence, you must produce a fit note stating that you are “not fit for work” or “may be fit for work” and the reason(s) why.  This should be forwarded to the practice manager immediately on receipt and date-wise should run from the 8th day of your sickness absence and be backdated if necessary by your GP/Medical advisor.  If your absence continues beyond the date the first fit note expires, further fit notes must be provided to run consecutively to cover the additional dates. You are therefore required to ensure that you make an appointment to see your GP/Medical advisor just before any current fit note expires and that this follow-on fit note is provided to the Company in a timely manner so that the certification of your sickness absence is continuous. Continued failure to provide proper certification of your sickness absence will be regarded as unauthorised absence, which is unpaid and may be subject to disciplinary action on your return to work.

Private Medical Certificate

proper certification of your sickness absence will be regarded as unauthorised absence, which is unpaid and may be subject to disciplinary action on your return to work.

Private Medical Certificate

The Company reserves the right to request you obtain a fit note for shorter periods of absence if deemed appropriate. For example you may be asked to produce a fit note if the absence precedes or follows a period of authorised annual leave; falls before or after a bank/public holiday, or if your behaviour at work suggests you are not fit for work or may not be receiving adequate medical attention.  Depending on the circumstances, the Company may reimburse you the cost of obtaining these certificates.

Fit to Work Medical Certificate – “Not fit to work”

If you are provided with a fit note, stating that you are “not fit to work”, then you should refrain from working for the duration of the period specified by the GP/Medical advisor. You may however be required to attend a welfare meeting during this period to discuss your absence.

you should refrain from working for the duration of the period specified by the GP/Medical advisor. You may however be required to attend a welfare meeting during this period to discuss your absence.

If you feel that you have recovered significantly to enable you to return to work before the end of the specified period on the note you should discuss this with your GP/Medical advisor. If your GP/Medical advisor agrees that you are ready to return, either to full or to adapted duties, you should contact the practice manager (by telephone), who will seek to arrange a welfare meeting as soon as possible, so the return can be discussed in detail.  Following the meeting if it is agreed that it is safe for you to return to work then a date for the return will be confirmed.  This may be the same day, the next available working day or another suitable day.

the meeting if it is agreed that it is safe for you to return to work then a date for the return will be confirmed.  This may be the same day, the next available working day or another suitable day.

The opportunity to return early may depend on the reason for the absence, your job role, working environment and potential risks returning early may have. Each situation will be considered on a case by case basis. You will only be permitted to return early from sickness absence if you can produce supporting medical evidence that you are or may be fit for work in the form of an updated fit note. If you are unable to provide this then you will not be permitted to return to work until your original fit note/medical certificate has expired.

Fit to Work Medical Certificate – “May be fit to work”

If you are provided with a fit note, stating you “may be fit to work” with adaptations, you should contact the practice manager immediately on receipt, to arrange a welfare meeting as stated above. The practice manager will consider what, if any, additional measures may be required to support your return to work, taking into account the GP/Medical advisors advice.

meeting as stated above. The practice manager will consider what, if any, additional measures may be required to support your return to work, taking into account the GP/Medical advisors advice.

If the Company is unable to accommodate any suggested adaptations by the GP/Medical advisor, or they feel it is not safe for you to return to work, then you will be considered “unfit for work” and should refrain from working. Further guidance may be required from your GP or a qualified medical practitioner to ascertain what, if any, reasonable adjustments may be required in order for you to return to work. This would be clearly discussed with you and you would remain off work in line with your fit note, until such adaptations can be accommodated or you are fit to return to work.

Notification of Infectious Diseases

You must notify the Company if you are suffering from or have symptoms of a notifiable infectious disease i.e. mumps, measles, or food poisoning etc., or where you have been in close contact with someone with such an illness.  Where you have been off work with this type of illness, you must contact the Company and your doctor prior to returning to work to ensure that it is safe to do so.

with someone with such an illness.  Where you have been off work with this type of illness, you must contact the Company and your doctor prior to returning to work to ensure that it is safe to do so.

If you are sick due to sickness and diarrhoea, you will be required to remain off work for 48 hours following your last episode and normal sickness arrangements apply.

Accidents at Work

If you are off sick following an accident at work the absence will be recorded in the usual way. All accidents should be reported immediately to management and be entered into the accident book as necessary.

Payment for absence following an accident at work are made in accordance with your Statement of Terms and Conditions of Employment. If you have an insurance policy which requires confirmation of loss of earnings due to the accident, this should be forwarded to the practice manager.

Absence Preceding or Following a Holiday

If you have an insurance policy which requires confirmation of loss of earnings due to the accident, this should be forwarded to the practice manager.

Absence Preceding or Following a Holiday

If you are absent (due to sickness) the day before or after a holiday or Bank/Public Holiday you may be required to submit a private medical certificate at your own expense. Failure to do so may result in the absence being treated as an unauthorised absence, may be unpaid and you may be subject to disciplinary action.  If you have difficulties in obtaining a private medical certificate, following a visit to your GP/Medical advisor, you should discuss this with the practice manager.

If you are absent for any other reason, you should discuss this with the practice manager at the earliest opportunity and provide suitable evidence to support your reason.  For example, change of flight details showing delay in pre-booked travel arrangements.

Absence during Annual Leave

at the earliest opportunity and provide suitable evidence to support your reason.  For example, change of flight details showing delay in pre-booked travel arrangements.

Absence during Annual Leave

If you fall sick during the course of annual leave, this may be converted to sick leave (and therefore sick pay) providing you request this on the first day of absence and you follow the absence policy.  The annual leave may then be taken at a later date, providing it is requested in line with the Company’s rules regarding booking annual leave.

In this situation, if the sickness commences during the annual leave, medical evidence will be required i.e. a fit note.  Any medical notes or letters, which are obtained while overseas, should be written in English (where possible) or translated by your normal GP/Medical advisor on your return.  In this case, both the original and translated note and letter should be submitted to the Company.  Copies can be taken, if required for insurance purposes.

If the absence is expected to last for more than 8 calendar days whilst abroad, copies of the medical notes should be scanned and sent electronically to the Company in order for the period of sickness absence to be authorised.

to last for more than 8 calendar days whilst abroad, copies of the medical notes should be scanned and sent electronically to the Company in order for the period of sickness absence to be authorised.

Any concerns regarding the authenticity of any documentation may lead to further discussion with you. If you are considered to have knowingly provided false documentation, you may be subject to action under the Disciplinary Procedure, which could include dismissal.

Conduct During Absence

The Company expects you to take the action most likely to facilitate a speedy recovery and return to work. You must act responsibly in dealing with your illness, seeking medical treatment as appropriate and must not engage in activities which could aggravate the injury or illness or delay your return to work.

Unauthorised Absence

Absence that has not been notified in accordance with this policy will be treated as “absence without official leave” (AWOL) and will be addressed as follows:

return to work.

Unauthorised Absence

Absence that has not been notified in accordance with this policy will be treated as “absence without official leave” (AWOL) and will be addressed as follows:

If you fail to notify the practice manager by the correct time, the Company will attempt to contact you.  This may be by telephone or in writing or via a third party such as your emergency contact/ next of kin.  The purpose of the contact is to understand the reason for the absence and non-contact.

If contact is unachieved, or the reasons provided are not satisfactory then the Company’s disciplinary procedure may be instigated and the period of unauthorised absence will be unpaid.

Part Day Absence

If you become ill during the working day and need to go home you should discuss this with the practice manager. The absence will be recorded as a part day absence and this will be broken down as follows:

If you attend work for more than two hours before leaving - this will be classed as a part day absence

If you attend work for less than two hours before leaving -  this will be classed as a full day absence

You will be required to complete the Company absence form upon your return to work and will be required to attend a return to work meeting.

Medical Appointments

be classed as a full day absence

You will be required to complete the Company absence form upon your return to work and will be required to attend a return to work meeting.

Medical Appointments

All appointments should be arranged where possible in your own time.  If this is unavoidable and you require time off work, you should notify the practice manager as soon as the appointment has been made, you may then be permitted to attend the appointment providing:

You have an appointment card or letter confirming your appointment

The appointment time will cause as little disruption as possible to the business e.g. at the beginning or end of the day

If the appointment has been arranged over the telephone, which would result in you having no appointment card, you must obtain evidence at the appointment and provide this to the practice manager as soon as possible.

Time off for the appointment will be unpaid.

You may be required to make up the time missed whilst attending the appointment.

Return to Work Meeting

to the practice manager as soon as possible.

Time off for the appointment will be unpaid.

You may be required to make up the time missed whilst attending the appointment.

Return to Work Meeting

When you return to work following any period of sickness absence, including any part day absence, you should report to the practice manager at the start of your shift.  A return to work meeting will usually take place within 8 hours on your first day back.  If for any reason the meeting is delayed, another appropriate manager may conduct the meeting or the meeting may be postponed to the following working day with mutual agreement.  The meeting will help confirm that you have recovered and are fit for work.  The meeting will also capture any relevant information about the absence and you will be informed of any concerns regarding your attendance record.  The meeting will be held privately and a supportive manner will be adopted with the following objectives.

To acknowledge your return and to investigate the reason for your absence and confirm the nature and extent of your illness or injury

To confirm that you have recovered and to gather as much information about the absence to ensure appropriate support or assistance can be offered

the nature and extent of your illness or injury

To confirm that you have recovered and to gather as much information about the absence to ensure appropriate support or assistance can be offered

To discuss any information provided on a GP fit note (if applicable) and advise on next steps if adjustments are to be made

To discuss any medication and potential side effects

To understand if the absence is likely to be repeated and what can be done to prevent further reoccurrence

To inform you of your current levels of absence

To obtain from you any completed documentation relevant to your absence i.e. absence form and / or GP fit note if not already submitted

To update you on events at work during your absence

To answer any questions you may have

To complete the Return to Work Form and obtain your signature on this.

Where necessary:

To discuss any concerns there may be with your attendance record

To discuss any trends or patterns in your attendance

Seek an explanation from you regarding any concerns or patterns

To inform you if your current absence levels have hit an absence management trigger point and agree next steps

To remind you of the impact of your absence levels on the Company.
